title: Golem (GLM) Price Forecast & Scenarios
content:
Analyzing Golem (GLM) requires an objective look at recent performance metrics alongside broader market trends. As of April 29, 2026, the token displays the dynamics of a mature decentralized infrastructure asset operating within a neutral and stable global market environment. | Metric | Details | |

---

|

---

| | Current Price | $0.13530600 | | Market Cap | $135.30M | | Market Rank | #230 | | 24H Change | -0.24% | | Analysis Date | April 29, 2026 |

## Recent Market Action Golem has experienced significant downward pressure recently, reflecting the broader volatility in the AI and DePIN sectors. The 30-day performance shows a high of $0.167538 and a low of $0.122635, representing a -24.27% change. Over the past year, the token has ranged from a high of $0.329537 to a low of $0.122635, marking a 55.54% decline from its yearly average. When compared to peers like Render and BitTorrent, Golem's price action highlights the sensitivity of decentralized compute tokens to market-wide risk sentiment.

## Key Price Levels The historical context for GLM shows a significant gap from its all-time high of $1.32. Currently, the $0.122 range acts as a critical support level, having been tested during recent market cycles. Resistance levels are established near the 30-day average of $0.136311, with additional psychological hurdles at the $0.20 and $0.30 marks.

## Valuation Scenarios Market participants should consider three scenarios when evaluating GLM's potential: 1. Bear Case: If the broader market experiences a correction and demand for decentralized compute shifts toward established competitors like Render, GLM may re-test its historical lows. 2. Base Case: Continued, incremental adoption of the Golem platform leads to price stabilization within its recent 30-day range, tracking the growth of the overall AI infrastructure sector. 3. Bull Case: A surge in institutional interest in decentralized AI infrastructure could drive increased utility for the GLM token, potentially testing the 1-year high of $0.329537.

## Risk Profile Golem is currently assigned a Risk Score of 8, classified as high risk. This reflects both the technical complexity of the project and its exposure to intense market competition. Investors should be aware that volatility remains a constant factor for assets in this category.

## Sector Comparison When compared to the wider cryptocurrency market, Golem's performance is intrinsically linked to the narrative strength of the DePIN and AI sectors. While tokens like BitTorrent occupy specific niches in file storage, Golem's focus on decentralized compute places it in a competitive environment with high technical standards and performance expectations.
